pullRequest.title: Refactor `test_starters` into smaller test files
pullRequest.body:
## Description
Used the `composer` agent from Cursor to refactor `test_starters.py` into smaller test files. I used the proposed division from https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/3594.

## Development notes

**Structure changes**
- Moved starter tests into `tests/framework/cli/starters/`
- Split test_starters.py into:
   - test_starters_flow.py – parsing, starter list, starter-specific behaviour
   - test_new_from_cli_flags.py – project creation via CLI flags
   - test_new_from_config_file.py – project creation via config file
   - test_new_from_prompts.py – project creation via interactive prompts

**Fixtures and helpers**
- Added `template_in_cwd` to avoid repeated shutil.copytree
- Added `_default_config()` for shared config in config-file tests
- Removed duplicate fixtures and rely on parent conftest
- Extracted `_create_example_pipeline_files()` from patch_cookiecutter_args
- Removed unused `mock_env_vars` and `_clean_up_project`

**Security**
- Addressed CodeQL “Incomplete URL substring sanitization” by replacing "github.com" in url with _is_github_host() using urllib.parse.urlparse to validate the hostname

pullRequest.title: Fix incorrect use of cwd for logging
pullRequest.body:
## Description
Addresses https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/3902

## Development notes
Specifically addresses the points made as follows:

> (Relatively minor) If I'm running a packaged project, I'd expect the logs to draw the project name from the project itself (for example using the package name, or the distribution name)

Changed how the package name is determined and added a test to verify the behaviour is now as expected.

> (Potentially more severe) Is there anything in the implementation that is mistakenly trying to use the pyproject.toml from disk when running a packaged project? Because that shouldn't happen as far as I understand.

Investigated with GPT5.1-Codex - verified by me:

Short answer: aside from project discovery, nothing in the packaged execution path tries to use a `pyproject.toml` from disk.

Details:
- `find_kedro_project(Path.cwd())` ([used in `KedroSession.__init__`](https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/main/kedro/framework/session/session.py#L114)) will read `pyproject.toml` in the current directory or its parents to detect a Kedro project. That’s the only place the file is consulted in this flow. If you start a packaged run from an unrelated directory that happens to contain a Kedro-style `pyproject.toml`, it could be picked as the `project_path` and therefore affect where the session store and default `conf_source` point, but it won’t override the package name or settings pulled from the installed package. Note that when running Kedro from a packaged project you need to always specificy the `--conf-source` anyway if you're executing it outside of the project directory. 
- [`configure_project(package_name)`](https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/main/kedro/framework/project/__init__.py#L300) and [`validate_settings()`](https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/main/kedro/framework/project/__init__.py#L326) operate solely off the installed package (`PACKAGE_NAME`), not off `pyproject.toml`.
- No other code in the packaged run path reads `pyproject.toml`.

## Description
<!-- Why was this PR created? -->

Related to: https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/4098

Users have encountered confusing behaviour when defining hook implementations with default parameter values. Due to how pluggy passes arguments, parameters with defaults receive the default value instead of the actual value passed by Kedro. 

This PR adds a warning to the hooks documentation to make this limitation explicit.

pullRequest.title: preserve runtime logging handlers in configure_project()
pullRequest.body:
## Description
To solve open issue: https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/4606

## Development notes
Here's a summary of what was changed and why:

kedro/framework/project/__init__.py

- _ProjectLogging.set_project_logging() — added preserve_logging: bool = False; the self.configure(self.data) call (which internally runs dictConfig) is now skipped when True. The package logger is still registered in self.data either way, so it's available for future use (e.g. _bootstrap_subprocess).

- configure_project() — added preserve_logging: bool = False and threads it through to set_project_logging(). Default is False so existing behaviour is completely unchanged.

tests/framework/project/test_logging.py

- test_configure_project_preserve_logging_keeps_runtime_handlers — verifies a custom runtime handler survives when preserve_logging=True, and that the package logger is still recorded in LOGGING.data.

- test_configure_project_overwrites_runtime_handlers_by_default — documents (and locks in) the existing default behaviour so we'd notice if it regressed.

pullRequest.title: Revert asyncio changes in async task execution
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Resolves https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/4611

## Development notes

There were asyncio changes introduced in #4562 to promote asynchronous paradigm and better readability. This introduced a bug in Kedro runners while using Kedro within an event loop (ex: Jupyter Notebook). This PR reverts the asyncio changes.

# QA notes

* All tests should pass
* **For manual testing -**

1. Create a pipeline which takes multiple inputs and produces multiple outputs as suggested [here](https://docs.kedro.org/en/stable/nodes_and_pipelines/run_a_pipeline.html#load-and-save-asynchronously)
```python
# async_test_pipeline/pipeline.py
def create_pipeline(**kwargs) -> Pipeline:
    return Pipeline([
        node(
            func=process_ds,
            inputs=["companies", "shuttles"],
            outputs=["preprocessed_companies", "preprocessed_shuttles"],
            name="preprocess_node"
        )
    ])


# async_test_pipeline/nodes.py
def process_ds(companies: pd.DataFrame, shuttles: pd.DataFrame):
    return preprocess_companies(companies), preprocess_shuttles(shuttles)
```
2. Try executing the pipeline with `kedro run --pipeline=async_test_pipeline` and `kedro run --pipeline=async_test_pipeline --async`. Both should yield similar results
3. Try executing the pipeline within Jupyter Notebook -

```python
from kedro.framework.session import KedroSession
from kedro.runner.sequential_runner import SequentialRunner
with KedroSession.create() as session:
    session.run("async_test_pipeline", runner=SequentialRunner(is_async=True))
```

pullRequest.title: Fix tests for `spark.DeltaTable` dataset
pullRequest.body:
## Description

This PR fixes the failing tests for #964. It has been very tricky to debug, so I created this PR using `feature/databricks-deltatable-dataset` as base.

## Development notes

Below is the (wild) journey I took to get to the bottom of this one.

### 🧽   Cleanup the Spark test setup

The current Spark test setup is bloated and very hard to follow with too many indirections. To be completely honest, I don't think I fully understand the whole `the_real_getOrCreate` and `UseTheSparkSessionFixtureOrMock` business. It causes a lot of issues when trying to decipher what actually was breaking, so I removed them all.  Now the setup is dead simple:

* All spark tests have an autoused `spark_session` fixture by default provided in conftest.
* Tests that need specialised session, e.g. hive, can override this fixture by providing a fixture of the same name in its test module.
* No need for all the gc cleanup, JVM caches, etc. anymore.
 
Now it's all idiomatic pytest and just works.

```python
Exception: Java gateway process exited before sending its port number

	Server access error at url https://repos.spark-packages.org/io/delta/delta-core_2.12/1.0.0/delta-core_2.12-1.0.0.pom (javax.net.ssl.SSLException: Unexpected error: java.security.InvalidAlgorithmParameterException: the trustAnchors parameter must be non-empty)

	Server access error at url https://repos.spark-packages.org/io/delta/delta-core_2.12/1.0.0/delta-core_2.12-1.0.0.jar (javax.net.ssl.SSLException: Unexpected error: java.security.InvalidAlgorithmParameterException: the trustAnchors parameter must be non-empty)
```

It says it can't resolved delta core JAR because of an SSL error: 

```
javax.net.ssl.SSLException: Unexpected error: java.security.InvalidAlgorithmParameterException: the trustAnchors parameter must be non-empty
```

A quick Google search led me to SO and finally the fix:

```
sudo rm /etc/ssl/certs/java/cacerts
sudo update-ca-certificates -f
```

We should do this when we build `kedro-builder` image, but running it as part of a CI run works too for now.

### 🦠   Second bug: sbt (ivy2) and pytest-xdist's multi-processing don't nice together

This took _forever_ to find 😭  Below is an example log of the symptom:

```
:::: WARNINGS
		[FAILED     ] io.delta#delta-core_2.12;1.0.0!delta-core_2.12.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/delta/delta-core_2.12/1.0.0/delta-core_2.12-1.0.0.jar. Please retry. (102ms)

		[FAILED     ] io.delta#delta-core_2.12;1.0.0!delta-core_2.12.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/delta/delta-core_2.12/1.0.0/delta-core_2.12-1.0.0.jar. Please retry. (102ms)

	==== central: tried

	  https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/delta/delta-core_2.12/1.0.0/delta-core_2.12-1.0.0.jar

		[FAILED     ] org.antlr#antlr4;4.7!antlr4.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/antlr4/4.7/antlr4-4.7.jar. Please retry. (48ms)

		[FAILED     ] org.antlr#antlr4;4.7!antlr4.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/antlr4/4.7/antlr4-4.7.jar. Please retry. (48ms)

	==== central: tried

	  https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/antlr4/4.7/antlr4-4.7.jar

		[FAILED     ] org.antlr#ST4;4.0.8!ST4.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/ST4/4.0.8/ST4-4.0.8.jar. Please retry. (12ms)

		[FAILED     ] org.antlr#ST4;4.0.8!ST4.jar: Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length for https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/ST4/4.0.8/ST4-4.0.8.jar. Please retry. (12ms)

	==== central: tried

	  https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/ST4/4.0.8/ST4-4.0.8.jar

		::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

		::              FAILED DOWNLOADS            ::

		:: ^ see resolution messages for details  ^ ::

		::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

		:: io.delta#delta-core_2.12;1.0.0!delta-core_2.12.jar

		:: org.antlr#antlr4;4.7!antlr4.jar

		:: org.antlr#ST4;4.0.8!ST4.jar

		::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
``` 

At first glance, it looks similar to the first problem, but it's not. It's a failed download, instead of an unresolved dependency. It says it can't download `org.antlr#antlr4;4.7!antlr4.jar` from any of the official repositories, i.e. maven, spark-apckages, etc., which makes no sense, because trying any of the link by itself, e.g. https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/antlr/antlr4/4.7/antlr4-4.7.jar, works just fine.

So what's going on here? I have a hunch after reading the line `Downloaded file size doesn't match expected Content Length` -- maybe two processes were trying to download these files concurrently and they interfere with each other, e.g. they all download to the same location and override each other's result. Not sure if that's actually the problem, but the hunch was correct. Running tests sequentially work. The error above only appears when running tests with pytest-xdist using more than 1 process.

The solution I implemented, which is adapted from pytest-xdist's own recommendation, is to just block all processes when a process is creating the spark session using a file lock. Works like a charm ✨ 🎩

### 💣.  Icing on the cake: OOM 

Just when I thought fixing the previous two problems were enough, [another cryptic error message](https://app.circleci.com/pipelines/github/quantumblacklabs/kedro/3668/workflows/4be1f64a-77a2-4650-9de0-c6a05e4b290c/jobs/70108) appeared:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/circleci/miniconda/envs/kedro_builder/lib/python3.8/site-packages/py4j/java_gateway.py", line 1207, in send_command
    raise Py4JNetworkError("Answer from Java side is empty")
py4j.protocol.Py4JNetworkError: Answer from Java side is empty
```

Fortunately, there is more in the error log:

```
self = <multiprocessing.popen_fork.Popen object at 0x7fb558b74b50>
process_obj = <ForkProcess name='ParallelRunnerManager-1' parent=2669 initial>

    def _launch(self, process_obj):
        code = 1
        parent_r, child_w = os.pipe()
        child_r, parent_w = os.pipe()
>       self.pid = os.fork()
E       OSError: [Errno 12] Cannot allocate memory
```

Right, it's an OOM (out of memory). This is where CircleCI's new Resource tab comes in very handy:

![Screenshot 2021-12-03 at 01 49 47](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2032984/144530956-eb72c2a6-2737-40ad-911e-e08698a85e4b.png)

We indeed maxed out on RAM, so I bumb the executors to `medium+`.

Now it's all passing 😆

pullRequest.title: Bump click dependency to >=8.2.0
pullRequest.body:
## Description
This PR bumps the `click` dependency from `click>=4.0,<8.2.0` to `click>=8.2` to resolve compatibility issues with other packages (e.g., when deploying Kedro projects to Airflow). Click 8.2+ introduced breaking changes that required updates to our codebase and tests.

## Development notes

### Changes Made

**Dependency Update:**
- Updated `pyproject.toml` to pin `click>=8.2` (previously `click>=4.0,<8.2.0`)

**Source Code Changes:**
- Fixed mypy type errors by adding type aliases for deprecated `click.MultiCommand` and `click.BaseCommand` types
- Added `TYPE_CHECKING` guards in `kedro/framework/cli/utils.py` and `kedro/framework/cli/cli.py` to map deprecated types to `click.core.Group` and `click.core.Command`

**Test Updates:**
- Updated tests to expect exit code 2 (instead of 0 or both) when groups are invoked without subcommands, per click 8.2+ behavior
- Added explanatory comments in tests to document why exit code is 2
- Simplified test logic by removing conditional handling for different click versions
- Updated confirmation abort tests to handle both "Aborted!" (click 8.2+) and "Deletion aborted!" messages

- Updated `RELEASE.md` to document the dependency bump

- Groups invoked without subcommands now exit with code 2 instead of 0
- Invalid confirmation input with EOF triggers "Aborted!" instead of using default values
- Error messages route to stderr instead of stdout
- `click.MultiCommand` and `click.BaseCommand` are deprecated in favor of `click.core.Group` and `click.core.Command`

- ✅ All CLI tests pass (1670+ tests)
- ✅ Linting checks pass (ruff, mypy)
- ✅ Manual verification of CLI commands confirms correct operation with click 8.3.0
- ✅ Successfully created and tested a new Kedro project

Can't remove `importlib_metadata` yet because for python 3.9 `importlib.metadata.entry_points().select()` doesn't work which is used here: https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/5c33b7d43005203ab2213540ceffaff21007e4b2/kedro/framework/cli/utils.py#L332-L336

With importlib_metadata 5.0 and Python 3.12, entry_points always returns an EntryPoints object. ## Description
Fixes #5105

## Problem
Mermaid diagrams in the Kedro docs were difficult to read in dark 
mode. The diagram shapes used dark navy fills with dark text, 
resulting in very low contrast and poor readability.

Investigated the mkdocs-material mermaid theme switching options. 
The mermaid2 plugin does not automatically respond to the 
mkdocs-material palette toggle. Attempted JavaScript-based theme 
switching but found CSS overrides to be the most reliable solution 
for the mermaid2 plugin.

pullRequest.title: Tidy settings.py file
pullRequest.body: ## Description The current settings.py file has grown organically. In 0.18 it's important as the single entry point for a user to edit runtime configuration so we should make sure it's proper pukka, consistent and easy to use👌 Here's how I've tried to arrange it, inspired somewhat by [Django's settings.py file](https://github.com/django/django/blob/main/django/conf/global_settings.py): * comments in settings.py are short and sweet, just describing _what_ the variable being defined below is * further information, in particular default values and _why_ you might want to change the setting, are only in the docs (linked to in the settings.py docstring) * I've split settings into two categories, "Lifecycle" and "Configuration", just to help divide it up a bit Some of this (the division between what's in settings.py comments vs. in docs; the two sections) are kind of arbitrary and I'm not strongly wedded to how I've done it here at all. Please do tell me if it makes sense or if you'd prefer something else. Here's what the docs look like rendered: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49395058/154074799-d3d5be73-a4d0-40c7-b88f-121391662cb4.png) ## Checklist - [ ] Read the [contributing](https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) guidelines - [ ] Opened this PR as a 'Draft Pull Request' if it is work-in-progress - [ ] Updated the documentation to reflect the code changes - [ ] Added a description of this change in the [`RELEASE.md`](https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/blob/main/RELEASE.md) file - [ ] Added tests to cover my changes

pullRequest.title: Add warnings when Node returns value but outputs=None
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Resolves #5232

## Development notes

This PR adds validation for the `outputs=None` case in node._outputs_to_dictionary().
If a node function returns a value while `outputs=None`, Kedro now emits a clear, actionable warning explaining that the return value will be ignored and how to fix the node definition.

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Use "data engineering and data science" everywhere</issue_title>
> <issue_description>**Recap**
> 
> Since the conversations about this topic have occurred in multiple places and this PR combines discussion about the philosophy of the change with the particulars about where the change is made, then I shall recap the discussion here:
> 
> - It is believe that referring to Kedro primarily as a tool for data scientists may dissuade non-data scientists from using it
> - Since non-data scientists constitute a large population of potential Kedro users, then that may limit the overall adoption of Kedro 
> - Therefore a change has been discussed, in order to prevent referring to Kedro primarily as a tool for data scientists
> - (Avoiding the term “advanced analytics” is preferred)
> - The emerging consensus is for “data engineering and data science” as the preferable term (where space allows), and “data pipelines” (where there is not sufficient space)
> - Changing the messaging to this will lead to some inconsistent messaging (even on the same page) - however there is already a wide range of messaging in current Kedro material, so this change may not cause an overall increase in inconsistency (in fact it may even make things more consistent in certain places)
> 
> **Philosophical decision**
> 
> We will make the suggested change (to “data engineering and data science” / “data pipelines”), based on this reasoning:
> 
> - It seems likely that changing the messaging could increase interest in Kedro, and other similar products use “data engineer”
> - There seems to be little risk in increasing the inclusiveness of the language (and the original reason for anchoring on “data science” in order to align with the marketing approach of McKinsey at the time is no longer a driving factor)
> - There is an emerging consensus which presents a workable solution, and the term “data engineering” is currently prominent in the messaging of related tools
> - There is some support across the TSC to update the Kedro messaging, and none have expressed a strong objection
> 
> **Implementation**
> 
> The messaging must be changed across:
> 
> -  Website
> -  Docs
> -  GitHub 
> -  PyPI
> -  (Other locations)
> 
> This PR addresses the changes to the website.

## Description

Resolves #5317 

## Development notes

The preview feature allows nodes to define a `preview_fn` that generates visualizations of node execution results. Here's what was implemented:

- Added `PreviewPayload` as a data class which represents the structured output from preview functions
- Added an optional `preview_fn` parameter when creating nodes that accepts a callable and returns a PreviewPayload.

def my_preview_fn():

pullRequest.title: Pin cachetools to <7 to fix ParallelRunner multiprocessing regression
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Resolves #5348 , and #5347 

Nightly CI unit tests started failing after the release of cachetools 7.0.0 on Feb 1st.

cachetools 7.0.0 introduces a [breaking internal change](https://github.com/tkem/cachetools/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst)  by converting @cachedmethod wrappers to descriptors, as noted in the release notes. These descriptor wrappers are not picklable and break multiprocessing execution in Kedro’s ParallelRunner, causing hook events to be dropped and related tests to fail.

This change caps cachetools to <7 to restore CI stability while compatibility with cachetools 7 is investigated.

Also created a follow-up ticket to investigate longer term - https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/5351

## Description
<!-- Why was this PR created? -->
PROTOTYPE FOR https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/issues/5425

## 🚀 Prototype: KedroServiceSession (stateless multi-run session)

This PR introduces a first prototype of a new session abstraction and a stateless multi-run session implementation.

### ✨ Key changes

#### 1. Introduce `AbstractSession` (Added in #5424) 

#### 2. Add `KedroServiceSession` (prototype)
- New session type designed for **multiple runs per session**
- Supports **per-run `runtime_params` injection**
- Uses a **stateless approach**:
  - creates a fresh `KedroContext` and `DataCatalog` for each run
~~- Preloads pipelines once at session creation for faster execution~~ (will be done in Subsequent PR/issue)

#### 3. Design decisions (v1)
- No session store (`_store`) for now
- ~~`session_id`~~ + `run_id` used for versioning (only timestamp)
- API kept close to existing `KedroSession` to minimise friction

### 📌 Motivation

Current `KedroSession` enforces a 1:1 mapping between session and run.  
This prototype explores a session model better suited for:

- long-lived processes (e.g. services, APIs)
- repeated pipeline execution
- dynamic runtime parameter injection

### ⚠️ Notes

- Coverage for `KedroServiceSession` is temporarily excluded (testing to be added in a branched off PR)
- Some aspects (naming, versioning strategy, store support) are still under discussion


---

Closes #5427

pullRequest.title: Add support for Python 3.14
pullRequest.body:
## Description
<!-- Why was this PR created? -->

Adds Python 3.14 support to Kedro.

The most significant change here is that Python 3.14 changed the default multiprocessing start method on Linux from `fork` to `forkserver`. 

With `fork`, new worker processes are cloned directly from the parent. They inherit its full memory state, including any Kedro project configuration and open file descriptors. With `forkserver`, a server process is pre-spawned before any test or application code runs, and workers are forked from that server on demand. In this case, the server has none of the parent's runtime state. 

This required changes in two places. First, `Task._run_node_synchronization` only bootstrapped worker processes (configure_project, hook registration) when the start method was `spawn`. `forkserver` workers arrived unconfigured, so hooks were never registered and no hook callbacks fired. Second, `pytest-cov` does subprocesses by injecting a sitecustomize.py that imports and starts coverage in every child process. This works under `fork` (the child inherits a valid working directory) and under `spawn` (a fresh interpreter with a normal environment), but it crashes under `forkserver`.
 
When the server process runs sitecustomize.py, coverage calls os.path.realpath('.') during initialization and raises FileNotFoundError because the forkserver has no accessible working directory. The server crashes, and every test that creates a SyncManager or ProcessPoolExecutor gets an EOFError on the broken communication pipe. The fix was to bootstrap guard in task.py to include "forkserver" and force the start method to "spawn" in tests/conftest.py on Python 3.14+ (coverage handles spawn correctly, and it matches the behavior already validated on Windows and macOS). Also, updating the SKIP_ON_WINDOWS_AND_MACOS marker to also skip the fork-dependent tests when `forkserver` is active; and add "forkserver" as a valid KEDRO_MP_CONTEXT value so users can opt into it.

Worth noticing that pytest-cov [dropped support for multiprocessing 3 major releases ago](https://pytest-cov.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelog.html#id36), so some jankiness is to be expected.

Also took the  opportunity to change some leftover text that still claimed that Kedro supports Python 3.9.
